title: A ledger left open
body: Julius sets the counting-house book in front of you, open to a page of his own corrections. He says nothing about who the errors belonged to before he found them.

choices.text: Go through the figures yourself, line by line
Take his word for it and move on

title: The dyeworks apprentice
body: A cloth merchant offers good money for a rush batch of madder-dyed wool, no questions asked about how the colour is fixed so fast. The usual process wants another week the merchant won't wait for.

choices.text: Take the commission and cut the process short
Refuse — the vats are done properly or not at all

title: Word gets around
body: A wool-stapler who lost money to a dyer's shortcut last year hears you turned down easy coin rather than rush a vat. He brings his custom to the Charetty house instead.

title: Simon's courtesy
body: Simon de St Pol finds you on the Bruges quay and delivers a compliment shaped like an insult, loudly enough for the dockhands to hear.

choices.text: Answer with a joke of your own
Say nothing and let him talk

title: A dockside rumour
body: A stevedore at the London wharves offers, for a few pence, a name and a ship due in from Calais with a hold worth knowing about before the rest of the quay does.

title: The rumour proves true
body: The ship the stevedore named comes in exactly as he said, and the cargo it carries moves the London market before anyone else has heard of it.

title: Godscalc's counsel
body: Father Godscalc asks, without preamble, whether a bank's ledger and a man's conscience are kept in the same hand or two different ones.

choices.text: Tell him the ledger comes first

title: News from the Rialto
body: A broker at the Rialto steps, recognising the Charetty mark on your manifest, offers to talk further than the going gossip — for a price, and in private.

choices.text: Note the offer and move on

title: Simon remembers
body: A shipment that should have cleared Calais on schedule is delayed a week by a customs officer who owes Simon de St Pol a favour. Nothing can be proven; nothing needs to be.

choices.text: Absorb the loss and say nothing

title: A season's reckoning
body: The dyeworks is quiet for an evening. Marian asks what the last seven months have actually added up to, beyond the entries in the book.
